name: Utah Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Utah - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Division of Professional Licensing — Speech-Language Pathology
provider.parentOrganization: Utah Department of Commerce, Division of Professional Licensing (DOPL)

address.streetAddress: 160 E. 300 South, Salt Lake City, UT 84111
address.description: 160 East 300 South, Salt Lake City, Utah 84111

renewalCycle: Every 2 years (by September 30 of even-numbered years)

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Utah SLP license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: $100 initial. $52 biennial renewal — one of the lowest renewal fees in the country.

mainEntity.name: Is Utah part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does Utah allow telepractice for SLPs?
Does Utah have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Utah participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Utah without a separate endorsement application.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Utah?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Utah while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
